ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Continued To Sep-07-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

`Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. J. HARMOND HUGHEY, Esq. (Tel: 415-573-9515) for plaintiff Mohamed Karah (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. KEVIN R. MINTZ, Esq. of Rankin, Shuey, Ranucci, Mintz, Kampasona & Reynolds (Tel: 510-433-2600) for defendant Aleksandr A. Volkov.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALEKSANDR VOLKOV's Motion To Dismiss.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to dismiss is denied.

Defendant seeks a discretionary dismissal because plaintiff failed to serve him within 2 years of filing this action. (See CCP 583.420.)

CRC 3.1342(e) provides numerous factors that the court should consider when exercising its discretion to dismiss the case. The court determines that those factors weigh in favor of denying the motion. The COVID pandemic began during the 2-year period and reasonably delayed service as well as court proceedings. The court has recognized the propriety of tolling due to the pandemic in similar circumstances. (See, e.g., Emergency Rules 9 and 10.) Plaintiff's counsel experienced health problems and the death of co-counsel who was handling this case also contributed to the delay. (See H. Harmond Decl., pars. 3-6.) Plaintiff attempted to serve defendant numerous times starting in early 2021 to no avail and could only effectuate service in March of 2022. (D. Hughey Decl., par. 2.) Weighing everything, the court concludes that this fact pattern does not warrant dismissal.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
